title: Capability Progression Map Schema
description: Generic schema for capability progression maps across any subject. Covers strands where the same skill or process is practised at increasing sophistication over time — not concept dependency chains. Granularity varies by subject: year-by-year (e.g. Science H/I), band-level (e.g. DT Y7-8, Y9-10), or other. Sequences are linear and may skip levels where a capability is not explicitly addressed at that point.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this file contains and what subject/context it covers.

subject.description: Subject this map belongs to. e.g. 'Science', 'Digital Technologies', 'English'

curriculum.description: Curriculum framework this map is aligned to. e.g. 'ACARA v9', 'MYP'

levels.description: Keyed by level identifier — year level string (e.g. '8') or band string (e.g. '7-8'). Contains standards/descriptors for that level. Only levels with explicit descriptors need entries.

label.description: Human-readable label for this level. e.g. 'Year 8' or 'Years 7-8'

standards.description: List of capability descriptors for this level.

code.description: Descriptor code. e.g. 'AC9S8H01', 'AC9TDI8P01'. Must be unique across all levels.

capability_goal.description: Interpreted capability endpoint at this level — framed as 'Students...'. Describes what students can DO at this maturation level, not what they know. Derived from the descriptor and elaborations.

sub_strand.description: Sub-strand this descriptor belongs to. Free string — subject-specific. e.g. 'questioning', 'planning', 'nature_of_science', 'creating_solutions', 'investigating'. Used to group descriptors into capability threads.

capability_threads.description: Capability maturation chains organised by strand and sub-strand. Each thread is a linear sequence of descriptor codes ordered from earliest to latest level. No branching. Gaps in the sequence are valid — a sub-strand may not have an explicit descriptor at every level.

label.description: Human-readable strand name. e.g. 'Science as a Human Endeavour', 'Science Inquiry', 'Design Process'

sub_strands.description: Keyed by sub-strand identifier. Each contains a linear sequence of codes.

label.description: Human-readable sub-strand name. e.g. 'Questioning and predicting', 'Nature and development of science', 'Generating and designing'

sequence.description: Ordered list of descriptor codes from earliest to latest level. Linear, no branching. Gaps allowed — only include levels where the capability is explicitly addressed. The visualiser uses each code's level key to position it correctly regardless of sequence gaps.

_notes.usage: Used by the capability progression visualiser. Paired with a concept progression map (e.g. science_y_goals_map.json) for subjects that have both concept and capability strands. The two maps are complementary — one per mode.
_notes.progression_model: Capability threads are maturation sequences, not dependency trees. The same skill (e.g. 'planning investigations') deepens each year/band. Visualise as horizontal swim lanes per sub-strand, one node per level, spaced by actual level position.
_notes.granularity: Level keys are flexible. Science uses year-by-year ('7', '8', '9', '10'). DT uses bands ('7-8', '9-10'). Mixed granularity within a file is valid if the curriculum warrants it.
_notes.gaps: A sequence does not need an entry at every level. If a sub-strand has no explicit descriptor at Y9 for example, simply omit it from the sequence. The visualiser spaces by level position, not sequence index.
_notes.schema_naming: This schema file should be named capability_progression_schema.json and referenced by all subject capability maps.
